segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: That's really interesting so the fact that ...
segments.zh: 那真的很有趣，所以事实上……

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: Tom, could I ask you for some advice, please?
segments.zh: 汤姆，我能请教你一些问题吗？

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Yes of course, if you think I can help.
segments.zh: 当然可以，如果你觉得我能帮上忙的话。是关于什么的？

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: What's it about?

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: It's my first media studies assignment, and I'm not sure how to go about it.
segments.zh: 这是我第一份媒体研究作业，我不太确定该怎么做。你去年肯定做过。

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: You must have done it last year.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Is that the one comparing the coverage of a particular story in a range of newspapers?
segments.zh: 是那个比较不同报纸对某个特定故事报道的作业吗？

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: That's right.
segments.zh: 没错。

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Oh yes, I really enjoyed writing it.
segments.zh: 哦对，我真的很喜欢写那个。

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: So what sort of things do I need to compare?
segments.zh: 那我需要比较哪些方面呢？

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Well, there are several things.
segments.zh: 嗯，有好几个方面。比如，报道出现在报纸的哪一页就是个问题。

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: For example, there's the question of which page of the newspaper the item appears on.

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: You mean, because there's a big difference between having it on the front page and the bottom of page ten, for instance?
segments.zh: 你的意思是，比如登在头版和第十版底部差别很大，对吧？

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Exactly.
segments.zh: 没错。这能看出编辑认为这条新闻有多重要。还有篇幅——报道占了多少栏英寸，横跨多少栏。

words.def: 栏英寸（报纸篇幅计量单位）

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: And that shows how important the editor thinks the story is.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Then there's the size – how many column inches the story is given, how many columns it spreads over.

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: And I suppose that includes the headline.
segments.zh: 我想这还包括标题吧。

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: It certainly does.
segments.zh: 当然包括。这都是吸引读者注意力的一部分。

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: It's all part of attracting the reader's attention.

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: What about graphics – whether there's anything visual in addition to the text?
segments.zh: 那图表呢——除了文字之外有没有视觉元素？

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Yes, you need to consider those, too, because they can have a big effect on the reader's understanding of the story – sometimes a bigger effect than the text itself.
segments.zh: 是的，这些也要考虑，因为它们对读者理解故事有很大影响——有时甚至比文字本身影响还大。然后你需要看报道是如何组织的：它采用了什么结构？记住，很多人只读第一段，所以记者在开头放了什么？而且，比如说，如果对某事有不同意见，是否有一个观点出现在结尾附近，人们可能根本不会读到那里？

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Then you'll need to look at how the item is put together: what structure is it given?

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Bear in mind that not many people read beyond the first paragraph, so what has the journalist put at the beginning?

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: And if, say, there are conflicting opinions about something, does one appear near the end, where people probably won't read it?

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: And newspapers sometimes give wrong or misleading information, don't they?
segments.zh: 报纸有时会给出错误或误导性的信息，对吧？要么是故意的，要么是偶然的。我也应该关注这一点吗？

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: Either deliberately or by accident.

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: Should I be looking at that, too?

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Yes, if you can.
segments.zh: 是的，如果你能做到的话。比较不同版本的内容，并尽可能分辨哪些是真的、哪些不是。这涉及到一个非常重要的点：作者的目的是什么，或者如果他们有多个目的，至少找出最重要的那个。表面上可能是在告知公众，但往往是为了制造恐惧、争议，或者让某人显得可笑。

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Compare what's in different versions, and as far as possible, try and work out what's true and what isn't.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: And that relates to a very important point: what's the writer's purpose, or at least the most important one, if they have several.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: It may seem to be to inform the public, but often it's that they want to create fear, or controversy, or to make somebody look ridiculous.

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: Gosh, I see what you mean.
segments.zh: 天哪，我明白你的意思了。而且我想作者可能会对读者做出一些假设。

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: And I suppose the writer may make assumptions about the reader.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: That's right – about their knowledge of the subject, their attitudes, and their level of education, which means writing so that the readers understand without feeling patronised.
segments.zh: 没错——关于他们对主题的了解、他们的态度以及教育水平，这意味着写作时要让读者理解而不感到被轻视。所有这些都会影响故事的呈现方式。

words.def: 以高人一等的态度对待

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: All of that will make a difference to how the story is presented.

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: Does it matter what type of story I write about?
segments.zh: 我写什么类型的报道有关系吗？

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: No – national or international politics, the arts ...
segments.zh: 没有——国内或国际政治、艺术……任何题材都行，只要在两三份报纸上有报道。当然，如果内容是你感兴趣且有所了解的，会更容易也更有趣。

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Anything, as long as it's covered in two or three newspapers.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Though of course it'll be easier and more fun if it's something you're interested in and know something about.

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: And on that basis a national news item would be worth analysing – I'm quite keen on politics, so I'll try and find a suitable topic.
segments.zh: 这样的话，一个国内新闻事件就值得分析——我对政治挺感兴趣的，所以我会试着找个合适的主题。汤姆，你分析时选了什么？

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: What did you choose for your analysis, Tom?

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: I was interested in how newspapers express their opinions explicitly, so I wanted to compare editorials in different papers, but when I started looking, I couldn't find two on the same topic that I felt like analysing.
segments.zh: 我对报纸如何明确表达观点感兴趣，所以想比较不同报纸的社论，但当我开始找的时候，发现找不到两篇同一主题且我想分析的。

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: In that case, I won't even bother to look.
segments.zh: 那样的话，我连找都不找了。

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: So in the end I chose a human interest story – a terribly emotional story about a young girl who was very ill, and lots of other people – mostly strangers – raised money so she could go abroad for treatment.
segments.zh: 所以我最后选了一个人情故事——一个关于重病小女孩的特别感人的故事，很多其他人——大多是陌生人——筹钱让她去国外治疗。其实我挺惊讶的——有些报纸只报道大家有多好，但其他的则考虑了更广泛的层面，比如为什么这里无法提供治疗。

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Actually, I was surprised – some papers just wrote about how wonderful everyone was, but others considered the broader picture, like why treatment wasn't available here.

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: Hmm, I usually find stories like that raise quite strong feelings in me!
segments.zh: 嗯，我通常发现这样的故事会让我产生很强烈的情绪！我会避开。也许我会选一个艺术主题，比如对一部电影的不同评论，或者关于艺术资助的事情——我再想想。

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: I'll avoid that.

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: Perhaps I'll choose an arts topic, like different reviews of a film, or something about funding for the arts – I'll think about that.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Yes, that might be interesting.
segments.zh: 对，那可能挺有意思的。

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: OK, well thanks a lot for your help, Tom.
segments.zh: 好的，非常感谢你的帮助，汤姆。真的很有用。

segments.speaker: HAZEL
segments.en: It's been really useful.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: You're welcome.
segments.zh: 不客气。祝你作业顺利，黑兹尔。

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Good luck with the assignment, Hazel.

items.prompt: What Hazel should analyse about items in newspapers: what 21 ________ the item is on

pairs.p: which page of the newspaper the item appears on
pairs.note: 题干问的是'item is on'什么，原文直接说'which page...the item appears on'，所以答案是page。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中'what...the item is on'对应原文'which page...the item appears on'，定位到'page'。

items.prompt: the 22 ________ of the item, including the headline

pairs.note: 题干中的22是题号，答案词'size'在原文中直接出现。
题干中的'the item'对应原文中的'the story'，指同一篇文章。

pairs.p: how many column inches the story is given
pairs.note: 题干说'包括标题'，原文解释'size'是文章占用的栏英寸数，标题是文章的一部分。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干中的'the 22'定位到原文的'size'，同时'the item'与'the story'同义替换，'including the headline'对应原文对'size'的解释。

pairs.p: anything visual in addition to the text
pairs.note: 题干问'随物品附带的任何23____'，原文说'除了文字之外是否有任何视觉内容'，'anything visual'对应'any ____'，'accompanying the item'对应'in addition to the text'。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句直接回答题干问题，'anything visual'就是答案'graphic(s)'的同义替换。

items.prompt: the 24 ________ of the item, e.g. what's made prominent

pairs.note: 题干问第24空，原文直接出现'structure'，与答案完全一致。
题干和原文中'item'完全对应，指同一事物。
题干'what's made prominent'（什么被突出）与原文'what structure is it given'（被赋予什么结构）同义，都询问文章的结构安排。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'look at how the item is put together: what structure is it given?'，其中'structure'直接对应题干中的24空，因此答案就是structure。

pairs.note: main 和 most important 是同义替换，都表示'主要的'。
题干和原文中完全相同的词，直接定位。
答案词在原文中直接出现，是填空的答案。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问作者的主要什么，原文中'what's the writer's purpose'直接给出了答案，且'most important'替换了'main'。

items.prompt: the 26 ________ the writer may make about the reader

pairs.q: the 26 ________ the writer may make about the reader
pairs.p: the writer may make assumptions about the reader
pairs.note: 题干和原文几乎完全一致，只是空格处填'assumptions'。

paraphrase.explain: 题干与定位句结构相同，直接找到原文对应句子，空格处即为答案。

pairs.note: 原文中直接出现 'a national news item'，与题干完全一致。

paraphrase.explain: 题干 'national news item' 在定位句中直接以 'a national news item' 出现，因此可以快速定位到该句。

pairs.note: 题干editorial是单数，原文editorials是复数，但词形相同，直接对应。

paraphrase.explain: 题干关键词editorial在原文中以复数形式editorials出现，直接定位到该词所在的句子。

pairs.p: stories like that raise quite strong feelings in me
pairs.note: human interest 指能引起情感共鸣的故事，原文用 'raise strong feelings' 解释其特点。

paraphrase.explain: 题干 'human interest' 对应原文中 'stories like that raise quite strong feelings'，通过情感反应定位到说话人拒绝该话题。

pairs.note: 题干arts直接对应原文arts topic，是原词复现。

paraphrase.explain: 题干arts在原文中以arts topic出现，直接定位到该处。
